checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only information derived from the source presentation. There are no fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections. The metadata title is an acceptable AI-generated field.
There are no fabricated URLs or contact details. The URL present in the source is partially omitted in the output (checked under C5), but no incorrect information has been invented.
All three slides from the source PDF are represented in the output JSON sections. While content within them is missing (see C5), the sections/pages themselves are accounted for.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are significant omissions in the output. On Slide 2, the URL 'data.nconemap.com' associated with the 'Geoportal Website:' is missing. More critically, on Slide 3, all nested list items under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' are absent. The source includes 10 detailed sub-bullets (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Website redesign/integration', etc.) that are completely missing from the JSON, representing more than 75% of the content for that slide.
checks.b_evidence_source: On Slide 3: 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data between web servers', 'Replicate all map/image services between data centers', 'Replicate geodatabases between DB servers', 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab', 'Website redesign/integration', 'NC OneMap', 'Geoportal'. On Slide 2: 'data.nconemap.com'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in section 3 only contains top-level items: 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'. Section 2 contains the paragraph 'Geoportal Website:' but excludes the URL.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 2 and 3, corresponding to Slides 2 and 3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted accurately reflects the source wording without altering meaning.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed both the source PDF and the output JSON. Each unique piece of content in the source appears only once in the corresponding sections of the output. No paragraphs or list items have been duplicated.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. Page titles (NC OneMap Update, Data Releases, Tasks) are correctly assigned as H1. On page 2, 'Natural Heritage Program' is assigned H2, which correctly nests under the slide title.
The source document consists of presentation slides with text and lists; there are no tables to evaluate.

The reading order generally follows the source. On page 2, the footer information ('Geoportal Website:') is moved before the main list. Per the instructions, minor reordering of footer/metadata for accessibility is an acceptable change and not a defect.

On page 3 ('Tasks' slide), the source contains a complex three-level nested list. The JSON output fails to capture the nesting and, more significantly, loses almost all of the list content. It only includes the two top-level items ('Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'), omitting all sub-points such as 'Infrastructure buildout', 'EPA parcels project implementation', etc. This is a major failure in preserving the document's structure and meaning.
checks.b_evidence_source: The nested list on page 3 includes items: Ongoing -> Infrastructure buildout -> Replicate data..., Replicate all map/image..., Replicate geodatabases...; EPA parcels project implementation; Upcoming -> Elevation download..., Website redesign... -> NC OneMap, Geoportal.

checks.a_analysis: On page 2, the source contains a URL: 'data.nconemap.com'. The JSON output includes the label 'Geoportal Website:' but completely omits the actual link/URL text that follows it.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to render the visual hierarchy and complete content of the 'Tasks' section from Page 3 of the source PDF. In the PDF, the 'Tasks' section includes detailed sub-bullets under 'Ongoing' (Infrastructure buildout, EPA parcels project implementation) and 'Upcoming' (Elevation download, Website redesign/integration). In the HTML screenshot, only the top-level 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' bullets are visible, and all nested information is absent. Additionally, the 'Geoportal Website' section on Page 2 of the PDF contains a specific URL ('data.nconemap.com'), which is missing from the corresponding section in the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 of the PDF shows nested bullets under 'Ongoing' (Infrastructure buildout, EPA parcels project implementation) and 'Upcoming' (Elevation download, Website redesign/integration). Page 2 shows 'data.nconemap.com'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows 'Tasks' with only two empty bullets for 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'. The URL 'data.nconemap.com' is missing after 'Geoportal Website:'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Data Releases' and 'Tasks' on page 1 of the HTML output.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; it consists entirely of text-based slides and list items.

The source PDF on page 3 uses three small green checkmark icons to indicate the status of specific tasks. In the HTML output, these meaningful icons are missing from their relative positions in the text. Instead, a single, oversized green checkmark icon is placed at the bottom of the section, divorced from its original context, which changes the visual meaning of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Three green checkmark icons positioned next to 'Replicate data...', 'Replicate all map...', and 'Replicate geodatabases...' on Page 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: A single large green checkmark image appears at the bottom of the document, with no checkmarks appearing within the 'Tasks' list.
checks.b_evidence_location: End of 'Tasks' section on page 1 of the HTML output.

checks.a_analysis: There is no visual evidence of duplicated body content. The appearance of headings in the Table of Contents ('Contents' box) and again in the main body is an expected accessibility feature and not a defect.
